description: Golden turn-count corpus. Real session JSONLs (copied/redacted from ~/.claude/projects) with hand-verified expected turn counts per tuglaws/turn-metric.md (Spec S01). Both the tugcast Rust scanner contract test and the tugcode segmenter contract test assert against these same numbers. Redacted fixtures had string leaves >120 chars truncated; redaction was proven faithful by checking the S01 classification of every user record is unchanged from the source. 'compact.jsonl' is a contiguous real slice spanning a /compact boundary. Counts mirror tugcode handleUserEntry's FULL rule: isMeta records, /compact summaries, slash scaffolding, and interrupt markers ([Request interrupted by user...] with permissionMode absent) are all excluded; genuine submissions and <task-notification> wakes count.
redaction: Strings longer than 120 chars truncated to their first 120 chars + a marker. Only payload bytes are trimmed; record types, isCompactSummary/isMeta/permissionMode fields, content block types, and all leading prefixes (scaffolding, task-notification, interrupt marker) that drive S01 classification are preserved verbatim.

fixtures.notes: Baseline: three genuine array submissions, nothing else. scanner == tugcode == 3.
Two genuine turns with a /model slash command between them. Three scaffolding records and one isMeta record must NOT count. scanner == tugcode == 2.
One genuine submission plus one <task-notification> wake opener (counts), with two tool_result echoes (excluded). scanner == tugcode == 2.

An essay prompt (permissionMode present -> genuine, counts) then a '[Request interrupted by user]' marker (permissionMode ABSENT -> sentinel, dropped). Only the prompt counts. Probes the [R07] dedup/orphan seam and the interrupt-marker exclusion. scanner == tugcode == 1.

Contiguous real slice spanning a /compact boundary: two genuine bare-string submissions count; one isCompactSummary continuation, two /compact scaffolding records, two isMeta records, and four tool_result echoes are all excluded. scanner == tugcode == 2.

22 genuine array submissions; five '[Request interrupted by user]' markers (permissionMode absent) are excluded. totalTurns=22. The reconcile test (Step 5) requests a deliberately small replay window so the frame's `count` (turnsCommitted within the window) is strictly less than totalTurns=22 — proving reconcile reads totalTurns, never count. The windowed count is asserted at replay time, not statically.
